meta.paper: FAPO: Fully Autonomous Prompt Optimization of Multi-Step LLM Pipelines

claims.claim_text: Multi-step LLM pipelines fail through interactions among retrieval, reasoning, and formatting steps, so prompt-only optimization can miss bottlenecks in the chain.

evidence.quote_text: Multi-step LLM pipelines fail through interactions among retrieval, reasoning, and formatting steps, so prompt-only optimization can miss bottlenecks in the chain.

claims.claim_text: FAPO beats the baseline GEPA in 15 of 18 model-benchmark comparisons.

evidence.quote_text: Across six benchmarks and three task models, FAPO beats the baseline GEPA in 15 of 18 model-benchmark comparisons.
FAPO wins 15 of 18 model–benchmark comparisons, with a mean gain of +14.1 pp.

claims.claim_text: The mean FAPO-GEPA gain is +14.1 percentage points across all comparisons.

evidence.quote_text: the mean FAPO–GEPA gain is +14.1 pp.

claims.claim_text: In prompt-first escalations to structural changes on HoVer and IFBench, FAPO wins all six with a mean gain of +33.8 pp.

evidence.quote_text: In the six HoVer and IFBench comparisons where prompt-first search escalated to structural changes, FAPO wins all six with a mean gain of +33.8 pp.

claims.claim_text: FAPO improves CTIBench-RCM test accuracy: +4.0 pp on GPT-5, +7.1 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Instruct, +2.0 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Reasoning.

evidence.quote_text: prompt-only FAPO lifts test accuracy by +4.0 pp on GPT-5, +7.1 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Instruct, and +2.0 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Reasoning.
Table 3: CTIBench-RCM optimization. All scores are exact-match accuracy (%)

claims.claim_text: FAPO uses Claude Code as its orchestrator optimization layer, separate from the task model being evaluated.

evidence.quote_text: FAPO uses Claude Code as its orchestrator optimization layer, separate from the task model being evaluated.

claims.claim_text: FAPO first attempts prompt-level optimization and escalates to structural changes only when attribution indicates prompt edits are not enough to resolve the dominant bottleneck.

evidence.quote_text: It first tries prompt edits and, only when prompt optimization appears insufficient, changes chain structure within the permitted scope when attribution identifies a structural bottleneck.
FAPO first attempts prompt-level optimization and escalates to structural changes only when attribution indicates that prompt edits are not enough to resolve the dominant bottleneck.

claims.claim_text: The optimization loop uses three core agents: optimization agent, step-attribution subagent, and variant-reviewer subagent.

evidence.quote_text: The optimization loop uses three core agents. The optimization agent reads the tenant playbook and scope contract then drives the optimization loop. The step-attribution subagent analyzes failures after each evaluation. The variant-reviewer subagent checks each proposal for scope compliance, placeholder integrity, data leakage, and scorer compatibility.

claims.claim_text: Three optimization levels are possible: prompt text, chain parameters, or chain structure.

evidence.quote_text: Currently three levels are possible: prompt text, chain parameters, or chain structure.

claims.claim_text: FAPO uses four guardrails against overfitting: split access controls, scope constraints, iteration memory, and variant immutability.

evidence.quote_text: Split access controls: The optimizer agent sees individual training cases. Validation and test expose aggregate scores only. Scope constraints: Tenant playbooks define allowed and forbidden changes. Iteration memory: A structured log records variants, scores, and exhaustion reasons. Variant immutability: Every accepted or rejected attempt gets a new variant file.

claims.claim_text: FAPO organizes optimization around a tenant model with isolated workspaces, ensuring reproducibility, isolation, and extensibility.

evidence.quote_text: We introduce this organizational structure to ensure reproducibility, isolation, and extensibility. All variants and scores are recorded in the tenant-level directories for full visibility into prior runs and optimization attempts.

claims.claim_text: On HoVer, FAPO extended the baseline 3-hop retrieval chain to 4-5 hops with multi-query BM25 search and entity-aware rescue.

evidence.quote_text: On HoVer, attribution identified insufficient retrieval coverage. FAPO extended the baseline 3-hop retrieval chain to 4–5 hops, with multi-query BM25 search and entity-aware rescue.

claims.claim_text: On IFBench, FAPO added deterministic post-processing nodes that enforce instruction constraints, producing gains of +19.84 to +38.95 pp.

evidence.quote_text: On IFBench, attribution identified format failures. FAPO added deterministic post-processing nodes that enforce instruction constraints. These changes produce gains of +24.78 to +48.56 pp on HoVer and +19.84 to +38.95 pp on IFBench.

claims.claim_text: FAPO wins 9 of 12 prompt-only comparisons, six with non-overlapping mean ± trial-standard-deviation ranges.

evidence.quote_text: On prompt-only comparisons, FAPO wins 9 of 12. We also consider CTIBench Root Cause Mapping
On the remaining benchmarks, optimization stayed at the prompt level. FAPO wins 9 of 12 prompt-only comparisons, six of which have non-overlapping mean ± trial-standard-deviation ranges

claims.claim_text: AIME is the only benchmark where GEPA leads FAPO across all three model comparisons; results are inconclusive.

evidence.quote_text: AIME is the only benchmark where GEPA leads FAPO across all three model comparisons; relative to the baselines, FAPO yields mixed results (-2.22, +3.78, and +1.55 pp across the three task models) that fall within the noise range

claims.claim_text: FAPO has higher run-to-run variation when prompt-first search escalates to pipeline changes due to path dependence in optimization trajectory.

evidence.quote_text: FAPO has higher run-to-run variation when prompt-first search is allowed to escalate to pipeline changes. This reflects path dependence in the optimization trajectory.

claims.claim_text: GPT-4.1-mini outperforms GPT-5.4-mini on four of six baselines due to asymmetric token budgeting (shared max_completion_tokens for GPT-5.4-mini).

evidence.quote_text: GPT-4.1-mini outperforms GPT-5.4-mini on four of the six baseline test benchmarks. Both models were given an identical 16,000-token generation budget, but the provider accounts for that budget differently

claims.claim_text: FAPO integrates the adversarial evaluate-analyze-propose-iterate search pattern from jailbreaking literature but changes the objective to mean score improvement across evaluation cases.

evidence.quote_text: We use this closed-loop search pattern, but change the objective from finding one successful failure case to improving the mean score of one pipeline variant across N evaluation cases.

FAPO is a constructive continuation of this search pattern. It keeps the evaluate–analyze–propose–iterate loop but changes the target to aggregate validation performance for one deployable pipeline variant.

claims.claim_text: The GEPA reproduction uses Claude Opus 4.6 as reflector, which may strengthen GEPA on HoVer and IFBench relative to reported scores.

evidence.quote_text: We use Claude Opus 4.6 through Bedrock with default settings as GEPA's reflector, replacing the original reflector model; this may strengthen GEPA on HoVer and IFBench relative to the reported scores.

claims.claim_text: The FAPO budget is limited to 50 variants or 10 optimization rounds per trial, whichever comes first.

evidence.quote_text: The FAPO budget is limited to 50 variants or 10 optimization rounds per trial, whichever comes first.
